title: adhatoda periplocifolia
intro: Adhatoda periplocifolia, also known as Vasaka or Malabar nut, is a plant species native to India and other parts of Asia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The medicinal properties of Adhatoda periplocifolia are attributed to its leaves, which are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating respiratory issues and other health conditions. The plant is known for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cough and cold symptoms, and even help manage asthma. Adhatoda periplocifolia is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
